title: In Memoriam: Paul Volcker
summary: Paul A. Volcker, an International Council member and longtime friend of the Belfer Center, passed away on December 8, 2019. He was 92 years old.
body: Paul A. Volcker Paul A. Volcker , an International Council member and longtime friend of the Belfer Center, passed away on December 8, 2019. He was 92 years old. A giant of a man both physically and in his contributions to society, he helped shape the American economy under five different presidents. Known for his independence and doing what was right for the economy despite political and popular pressures, Volcker is best known for ending a long period of high inflation after President Carter chose him as Fed chairman in 1979. In addition to fighting inflation, he worked during the 70s and 80s to limit the easing of financial regulations, noting that the rapidly increasing federal debt was a danger to the country’s financial health. From 2009-11, Volcker worked under President Obama as Chairman of the Economic Recovery Board that was formed in response to the 2008 financial crisis. In that role, he convinced policymakers to impose new restrictions on big banks—a move known as the “Volcker Rule.” One of Volcker’s lesser known achievements was his success in helping families of Holocaust victims reclaim deposits that Swiss banks had failed to return. He also investigated corruption within the UN’s Oil for Food program, prompting the President of the World Bank to ask him to lead a review of the Bank’s anti-corruption program, which led to substantial reforms in World Bank procedures. Belfer Center Director Ash Carter remembers Paul Volcker as a good man dedicated to his work. “Over a life spanning ten decades,” Carter said, “Paul showed the rest of us that doing the right thing—and sticking with it in the face of resistance—can build a better world as nothing else can.” Former Center Director Graham Allison , a friend of Volcker who also worked with him on the Center’s International Council, called him “the very model of a modern public servant.” The latest from Belfer See more Recommended citation "In Memoriam: Paul Volcker." Belfer Center Newsletter, Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School. (Spring 2020). result.main_content_en: Paul A. Volcker, a member of the Belfer Center's International Council, passed away in December 2019 at age 92. He was a towering figure who shaped the American economy under five presidents, renowned for his independence and commitment to sound economic policy. His most famous achievement was ending the high inflation of the 1970s as Federal Reserve Chairman. He also worked to limit financial deregulation, warning of the dangers of rising federal debt. Following the 2008 crisis, he chaired President Obama's Economic Recovery Board and championed the 'Volcker Rule' to restrict big banks' speculative activities. Other significant contributions included helping Holocaust victims' families recover deposits from Swiss banks and leading anti-corruption investigations at the UN and World Bank, which spurred major institutional reforms. Colleagues remembered him as a dedicated public servant and a model of integrity.
result.main_content_zh: 保罗·A·沃尔克是贝尔弗中心国际理事会成员，于2019年12月去世，享年92岁。他是一位在身形和对社会贡献上都堪称巨人的杰出人物，曾在五位美国总统任内塑造了美国经济。他以独立性和顶住政治与民意压力坚持正确经济政策而闻名，其最著名的成就是在1979年担任美联储主席后终结了长期的高通胀。此外，他在七八十年代致力于限制金融监管的放松，指出快速增长的联邦债务对国家金融健康构成威胁。2009年至2011年，沃尔克在奥巴马总统领导下担任经济复苏顾问委员会主席，以应对2008年金融危机。在此期间，他说服政策制定者对大型银行施加新的限制，即著名的“沃尔克规则”。他较为不为人知的成就包括成功帮助大屠杀受害者家庭追回瑞士银行未归还的存款，以及调查联合国“石油换食品”计划中的腐败行为，这促使世界银行行长邀请他领导对该行反腐败项目的审查，从而推动了世行程序的重大改革。贝尔弗中心同仁赞誉他为现代公务员的典范和致力于工作的正直之人。
